pr.title: Use HSD_PAD defines for held_inputs mask
pr.author: r-burns

summary: Replaced raw controller input bitmasks in fighter and item code with HSD_PAD_* defines, and tightened the Fighter input struct typing so input fields use HSD_Pad instead of plain s32. The PR also included small decomp cleanup that likely helped preserve or improve matching/readability: simplified boolean comparisons, removed redundant casts, used GET_FIGHTER/GET_ITEM helpers, replaced some magic motion IDs with named constants, and normalized float literals/argument ordering in touched code.
what_changed: Changed Fighter input subfields in src/melee/ft/types.h from s32 to HSD_Pad for x660, x664, x668, and x66C, with comments identifying x660 as previous held inputs, x668 as pressed inputs, and x66C as released inputs.

Replaced raw held_inputs and x668 button masks with named HSD_PAD defines across fighter input handling: examples include 0x100 to HSD_PAD_A, 0x200/512 to HSD_PAD_B, 0xC00 to HSD_PAD_XY, 0x60 to HSD_PAD_L | HSD_PAD_R, and 0x80000000 to HSD_PAD_LR.
In Fighter_Spaghetti_8006AD10, rewrote held input masking and synthetic L/R aggregation using HSD_PAD_A, HSD_PAD_XY, HSD_PAD_Z, HSD_PAD_L, HSD_PAD_R, and HSD_PAD_LR instead of hex constants.
Updated input timer checks in fighter.c to use named pad constants for A, B, X/Y, D-pad up/down, L/R, and combined L/R inputs.
Updated character-specific special move IASA/animation code for Donkey Kong, Game & Watch, Mewtwo, Samus, and Yoshi to use HSD_PAD_B, HSD_PAD_A, and HSD_PAD_LR instead of numeric masks.
Updated common fighter checks such as ftCommon_8007FFD8 and ftCo_800CE650 to use composed HSD_PAD masks like HSD_PAD_AB | HSD_PAD_XY | HSD_PAD_LR.
In itlinkhookshot.c, replaced A-button raw masks with HSD_PAD_A and removed several redundant HSD_GObj/Fighter_GObj casts around item/fighter gobj calls.

Applied local cleanup in touched files: simplified comparisons such as gm_8016B0FC() == 0 to !gm_8016B0FC(), used GET_FIGHTER instead of gobj->user_data in ftattacks4combo.c, changed allow_interrupt assignment to false, and replaced motion-state magic value 0x155 with ftLk_MS_AttackS42.
Normalized some float literals from lowercase f to uppercase F and simplified unnecessary casts in Game & Watch conditions.
smart_moves: Changing the struct field types to HSD_Pad made the pad-bit replacements type-backed rather than just cosmetic, making future raw-mask regressions easier to spot.
Used existing aggregate defines such as HSD_PAD_XY, HSD_PAD_AB, and HSD_PAD_LR where they match the original combined masks, avoiding ad hoc local expressions.

Preserved bitwise semantics while improving readability: examples include keeping x668 pressed-input checks as bitwise tests and rewriting held-input masks with &= rather than assigning parenthesized masked values.
Documented input field roles directly in Fighter::input comments, especially distinguishing previous held inputs, pressed inputs, and released inputs.

Opportunistically cleaned up surrounding decomp style without broad behavioral rewrites, such as replacing raw gobj->user_data with GET_FIGHTER and removing redundant casts where type information already sufficed.
decomp_lessons: For controller input code, prefer HSD_PAD_* defines over numeric bitmasks; this makes intent searchable and reduces the risk of misreading high-bit sentinel-style values such as 0x80000000.
When a field stores pad bits, type it as HSD_Pad in headers instead of s32 so downstream code naturally uses pad constants.
Use combined pad defines when the game treats multiple buttons as one logical input group, for example HSD_PAD_XY for X/Y and HSD_PAD_LR for logical shield/trigger grouping.

Boolean cleanup can help readability and may still preserve matching when done locally and carefully: `if (!gm_8016B0FC())` and `if (mask & HSD_PAD_B)` are clearer than explicit comparisons to 0 or false.
Prefer project accessor macros like GET_FIGHTER and GET_ITEM over direct user_data access when the surrounding codebase has established them.
Replace motion-state magic constants with named motion-state enum values when a reliable name is known, as with ftLk_MS_AttackS42.
naming_conventions: Use HSD_PAD_A for A-button masks instead of 0x100.
Use HSD_PAD_B for B-button masks instead of 0x200 or decimal 512.
Use HSD_PAD_XY for combined X/Y masks instead of 0xC00.
Use HSD_PAD_L | HSD_PAD_R for physical L/R trigger checks instead of 0x60.
Use HSD_PAD_LR for the synthesized/logical L/R mask instead of 0x80000000.
Use HSD_PAD_DPADUP and HSD_PAD_DPADDOWN for D-pad direction masks instead of raw 8 and 4.
Name Fighter input pad fields by role in comments where exact field names are still provisional: x660 as previous held inputs, x668 as pressed inputs, x66C as released inputs.
Use named motion-state constants such as ftLk_MS_AttackS42 when replacing known numeric IDs.
assembly_or_matching_tactics: Raw constants were replaced with macros that expand to equivalent bit values, preserving generated bitmask behavior while making source intent clearer.
Compound masks were expressed with bitwise OR of HSD_PAD defines, e.g. `HSD_PAD_A | HSD_PAD_XY` and `HSD_PAD_AB | HSD_PAD_XY | HSD_PAD_LR`, instead of opaque hex masks.
Simplified redundant casts and explicit boolean comparisons in touched functions, suggesting matching was maintained despite cleaner C expressions.
Used uppercase float suffixes such as 0.0F and 1.0F in several touched call sites, consistent with decomp style preferences visible in the diff.
No review discussion in the provided slice explains specific matching constraints; the evidence only shows the final source-level transformations.
review_feedback: No review comments were present in the provided excerpt.
The PR had one issue comment according to counts, but its body was not included in the provided context, so no reviewer intent can be extracted.
follow_up_queries: Search for remaining raw input masks like 0x100, 0x200, 0xC00, 0x60, 0x80000000, 8, and 4 in fighter input code.
Audit other Fighter::input fields for possible HSD_Pad typing and role comments.
Look for direct `gobj->user_data` access in fighter code where GET_FIGHTER would be clearer and still match.
Check item code for redundant HSD_GObj/Fighter_GObj casts after function prototypes have been typed more precisely.
Search for remaining motion-state numeric constants near known named enums, especially in Link and character-special code.
